display_name: 管流湍流临界振幅指数 γ
benchmark_scope: Derive the critical perturbation amplitude scaling epsilon_c(Re) ~ Re^{-gamma} for subcritical transition in Hagen-Poiseuille pipe flow from the incompressible NSE, obtaining the exact value of gamma without empirical input. Linear stability predicts no transition at all; the challenge is to derive the nonlinear threshold from energy balance.
planning_constraints: Do not use gamma = 1 or gamma = 3/2 as given input; the value must be derived from NSE structure.
Do not assume non-normality of the linearized operator without proving L*L != LL* from the definition.
Do not cite Landahl (1980) or G_max ~ Re^2 as given; if transient growth is used, derive the Re-scaling.
The energy gap between Re_E ~ 82 and transition Re ~ 2000 must be mechanistically explained.
Any scaling argument must explicitly track how Re enters both the production term and the dissipation term.
Nonlinear bounds must be explicit in ||u'|| and Re; do not assume Sobolev constants are O(1).
